rows.notes: Clear centered page title '1860' with manuscript heading 'Soluta Varia' immediately below a ruled line. Very legible.
Single-name line 'Keeley' visible at top of list. No clear monetary figures aligned on this row (looks like a simple entry or label); ink for amounts on this exact line is faint/absent.

Description 'Kidlington Cottages' is legible. Amount reads as '1 11 8 1/2' (pence has a trailing loop/'ob' interpreted as halfpenny). Vertical alignment of digits is clear and values pass currency validation.
Name 'Osborne' clear. Amount aligned reads '2 14 11' with digits well-placed between the pound/shilling/pence guides; digits are distinct and valid.

Description partially faded/peculiar spelling ('Munkenowt'/'Munkensowt') but likely a charging entry '... charge on collecting - Glubb'. Amount digits faint; read as '2 1 0' given alignment, but digit shapes are ambiguous (could be 1/2). Chosen reading fits column alignment and validation.

rows.description: In lieu of fine - Kidlington Miles
rows.notes: Longer description 'In lieu of fine - Kidlington Miles' readable. Amount '146 7 6' is large and clearly aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; digits are unambiguous.
'Prior Fraternity' readable; amount shows a clear '20' in the pounds column and no shillings/pence marks visible, interpreted as 20 0 0.
Description 'Hayne Painting' is legible. Amount reads '13 5' with no pence digits visible; taken as 13 pounds 5 shillings 0 pence. Alignment is clear.
Entry 'Gas Company' appears twice on page; this occurrence reads clearly as '35 4 4' in the three monetary columns. Digits distinct and valid.
Long phrase 'Parker affirmed loss of trade' readable. Amount in pounds column is a clear '100' with no shillings/pence shown; interpreted as 100 0 0.
This is a sub-note beginning with 'do' (same as previous subject). Amount reads '10 10' (10 pounds 10 shillings). The word 'Cathedral' is faint but discernible; monetary columns are well aligned.

rows.description: Lawrence, grant to. for obtaining sale of property, &c.
rows.notes: Description 'Lawrence, grant to. for obtaining sale of property, &c.' mostly legible. Amount shows a clear single '5' in pounds column, interpreted as 5 0 0.
Heading-like entry 'Wells' followed by indented sub-lines. No amount on this heading row; it is a parent for subsequent charged items (braced).

Two sub-items under 'Wells' are bracketed; the bracketed total is written as '42 3 8'. The description line includes 'hire of plate &c' and 'purchase of 36.3.8' (the latter looks like an internal breakdown). The total '42 3 8' aligns well and is readable.
Read as 'Haynes Instruction of Choir' (wording slightly abbreviated). Amount is a clear '30' in pounds column with no shillings/pence, interpreted as 30 0 0.
The words 'Chapel Fund' are very legible. Amount shows '15' (understood as 15 pounds 0 shillings 0 pence).
Longer description readable. Amount appears as '20 4 4' clearly placed in the three monetary columns.
Single-word heading 'Selchams.' (spelling uncertain) followed by indented expense lines (sub-items). No amount shown on the heading row itself.
Indented sub-item under 'Selchams.' reads 'Farm Gate & Hill'. Amount '14 12 1' visible and fits currency rules; digits read clearly.
Line reads 'Blacksmith wires' (some ink fade but legible). Amount reads '6 8 5' and aligns well in the monetary columns.
Single-word entry 'Gravel' with amount '56 19 6' clearly present. Shillings 19 is within valid range; digits are crisp.
Name looks like 'Coppock' or similar; text is somewhat faint and may be a heading for the following tax items. No amount on this line itself.
A header-like label for subsequent lines; appears as 'Ppr Tax' / 'Pop Tax' / 'Poor Tax' in the handwriting. Subsequent lines list dates/parts and amounts. This row has no direct amount.
Indented under the tax heading; reads '1/2 Mil 1859' with a clear amount '60 17 2'. Alignment and digits are legible.
Line reads 'Lady Day 1860' and amount '23 8 2' is reasonably clear; ink is slightly lighter but numeric alignment supports this reading.

Parenthetical 'Closets' with a large amount '335 7 2' that is very distinct. Pounds are three digits and clear; this appears to be a subtotal/large line item (kept as 'entry' because it is a charge line).
Name 'Tymms' legible. Amount reads as '4 4' (4 pounds 4 shillings); pence not shown. Digits smaller here and slightly uneven but consistent with column alignment.
'Embling' is readable though ink a little light. Amount appears to be '6 15' (6 pounds 15 shillings); chosen because the two-digit shilling cluster aligns in the shilling column.

There is another 'Reading room' lower on the page. The numeric cluster adjacent reads '6 15' but the handwriting for this particular line is faint and could alternatively be '2 10' depending on stroke interpretation; chosen reading based on vertical alignment with nearby amounts.
Reads 'Stove Morton Church' (or 'Stove, Morton Church'). Amount '2 10' is clear in the pounds/shilling columns and typical for this type of item.
Line reads 'Labourers. Gravel' (abbreviated). Amount '5' in pounds column readable though small; taken as 5 0 0.
Text 'Lee iron work Fellows garden' mostly legible. Amount '21 8' shown; pence column blank. Alignment and digit shapes are clear.
Large amount '359 13 1' aligned to the right and clearly legible. Description reads as 'Tymm Wooden Buildings & Quadrangle' (name spelling a bit uncertain but item clear).
Line interpreted as a half-year stipend '1/2 yearly Stipend, P.T.D.' (leading word uncertain - read as 'Self' or 'Telf'). Amount '73 8 9' is legible; digits align properly.

Description is somewhat cramped and abbreviations present; reads as porter-related duty for 1860. Amount shows '12' in pounds column; no shillings/pence shown. Ink around this line is lighter than surrounding lines.
Reads 'turning Stables into rooms' with the name 'Tymn' preceding; amount '46 1' visible (46 pounds 1 shilling). Pence column blank. Moderate legibility of letters; numbers clear.

Phrase reads awkwardly ('Cook Shoe Black Fraternity'); likely several words concatenated. Amount '10' in pounds column is clear. Because the wording is unclear, confidence reduced for description but numeric value is confident.
Name 'Buckridge' (spelling approximated) legible. Amount reads '24 10' (24 pounds 10 shillings) clearly in the monetary columns.
Second occurrence of 'Gas Company' on page; amount '91 12 6' is very clear (digits distinct and well aligned).

rows.description: Bursary of Jesus: House Rent & Rates
rows.notes: Reads 'Bursary of Jesus: House Rent & Rates' (handwriting slightly compressed). Amount '39 1 2' clearly written and aligns with the numeric columns.
Bottom-most ruled total line shows a clear final total '3852 10 5' (pounds/shillings/pence). Very legible and used as a column alignment reference; consistent with other numeric entries.
